classes.reasoning: This document is explicitly labeled as an 'EDITORIAL' published in the journal ONCOIMMUNOLOGY (2023, VOL. 12, NO. 1, 2175506) with a DOI (https://doi.org/10.1080/2162402X.2023.2175506). It discusses tumor-infiltrating lymphocyte therapy for melanoma and references multiple clinical trials and research studies.

intervention: tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs), ipilimumab, pembrolizumab, nivolumab, dabrafenib, trametinib
summary: This is an editorial article published in the journal Oncoimmunology (2023) discussing the use of tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs) for melanoma immunotherapy. The authors review recent clinical trial data comparing TIL therapy with ipilimumab and discuss the evolving landscape of melanoma treatment including immune checkpoint inhibitors and targeted therapies.
references_to: Rohaan et al. multicenter, open-label, randomized phase 3 clinical trial published in The New England Journal of Medicine
Steven Rosenberg and colleagues TIL therapy research
clinical trial enrolling 834 advanced melanoma patients comparing pembrolizumab vs ipilimumab
168 patients randomly assigned to TIL therapy or ipilimumab
